text:
"summary": "Re-adding nmp-nip60 to workspace.members now compiles a mandatory ureq TLS HTTP client + 409-line synchronous Cashu mint client (src/cashu/client.rs) into every `cargo build`/`cargo test --workspace`/CI run, even though the PR's own docs redefine the crate scope to 'NIP mechanics only — event codecs, types, pure shape validation' with all networking in the not-yet-existent nmp-wallet. Sibling HTTP-using crates (nmp-nip57, nmp-nip05) gate ureq behind an optional `native` feature (`native = [\"dep:ureq\"]`); nmp-nip60 makes it unconditional. Cheaper: gate ureq + cashu::client behind an optional feature, or move client.rs to nmp-wallet, so builds that do not exercise minting skip ureq + rustls/ring compilation.", [...] (1) DELETED code: `git diff master...pr2866` shows `crates/nmp-nip60/src/backend.rs` removed (82 deletions: the `WalletBackend` trait, `PayResult`, `WalletError`), plus `pub mod backend;` and `pub use backend::{PayResult, WalletBackend, WalletError};` removed from lib.rs. `ls backend.rs` = No such file. So the "seam surface consumed by nmp-wallet" phrase describes code that no longer exists in this crate after the PR.

(2) adapter-vs-seam conflation: design doc is explicit — line 101 "`WalletBackend` lives in `nmp-wallet`, not `nmp-core` and not `nmp-nip60`"; line 75 (Existing NIP Crates) nmp-nip60 owns "a Cashu backend adapter **for** the `nmp-wallet::WalletBackend` seam." The doc cleanly separates *adapter* (nip60's) from *seam* (nmp-wallet's). The claim's phrase "the Cashu backend adapter **seam surface** consumed by nmp-wallet" fuses the two and reads as claiming the seam — which the crate's own note[0] (ownership.rs:112-115) then contradicts: "the unified WalletBackend seam ... are owned by nmp-wallet ... not this crate." Internal tension between the `owns` text and its own note.
